 {"id":"bd-271","title":"Unit tests for config.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for config.sh Module

## Module Overview
config.sh provides XDG-compliant configuration management:
- `config_init` - Create default config files
- `config_load` - Load config into DSR_CONFIG array
- `config_get` - Retrieve config values with defaults
- `config_set` - Update config values
- `config_validate` - Schema validation
- `config_show` - Display configuration

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Initialization Tests
- `config_init` creates all required directories
- Creates default config.yaml with valid schema
- Creates hosts.yaml with default hosts
- Creates repos.yaml with empty registry
- Creates secrets/ directory with proper permissions (700)
- Does NOT overwrite existing config (unless --force)
- --force flag recreates config from scratch

### 2. Loading Tests
- `config_load` populates DSR_CONFIG associative array
- Missing config file triggers error with helpful message
- Malformed YAML produces clear error
- Default values applied for missing keys

### 3. Get/Set Tests
- `config_get` returns correct values
- `config_get` with default returns default when key missing
- `config_set` updates in-memory value
- `config_set --persist` writes to disk
- Nested key access (e.g., `signing.enabled`)

### 4. Validation Tests
- Valid config passes validation
- Invalid schema_version fails
- Missing required fields fail
- Type mismatches detected (string vs int)

### 5. XDG Compliance Tests
- Respects XDG_CONFIG_HOME override
- Respects XDG_CACHE_HOME override
- Respects XDG_STATE_HOME override
- Falls back to ~/.config, ~/.cache, ~/.local/state

## Test Approach
- Use TEMP_DIR for isolated testing
- Set DSR_CONFIG_DIR to temp directory
- Clean up after each test
- NO mocking - test real file operations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:40.632391316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:10:20.351497645Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:10:20.350701685Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-271","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:40.632391316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-283","title":"E2E: dsr status (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr status (real behavior)

## Purpose
Verify status output with real data sources (logs, cache, repos config) and JSON schema validity.

## Approach
- Run `dsr status` in a temp XDG state with seeded log files.
- If state is empty, ensure output is graceful and schema-valid.

## Logging
- Emit the temp XDG paths used.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr and relevant files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Human output includes last run summary when available.
- [ ] --json output matches schema and has empty stderr on success.
- [ ] Empty state handled without errors.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:55:22.561427223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:07:40.084311895Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:07:40.084293551Z","close_reason":"E2E status tests complete: 15 tests covering help, human output, JSON schema, empty state handling.  {"id":"bd-29b","title":"E2E: dsr build (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr build (real behavior)

## Purpose
Exercise build command paths without mocks, using real local repos and dry-run/plan modes when external deps are missing.

## Approach
- Use a temp repo with a minimal workflow file for act.
- If docker/act missing, run `--dry-run` and assert planned actions.
- If docker/act available, execute a tiny workflow and verify artifacts.

## Logging
- Record repo path, workflow file, and tool config.
- On failure, dump act logs and stdout/stderr.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dry-run produces deterministic plan output.
- [ ] Real run (when deps available) produces artifacts + manifest.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:55:43.935502571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:37.024551685Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29b","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:35.186637406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-29b","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:37.023320756Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Purpose
Validate repos subcommands using real repos.d config files without mocks.

- `dsr repos list`
- `dsr repos info <tool>`
- `dsr repos validate`
- `dsr repos discover --dry-run`

- Validate that list/info output matches fixtures.

- Print fixture paths and command lines.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr and repo files.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] --json output schema-valid and stderr clean on success.
- [ ] Errors on malformed YAML are clear and actionable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:55:32.489262223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:33.404083786Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kf","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:31.579405945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2kf","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:33.403724219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2lm","title":"Test infrastructure: run-all-tests.sh runner script","description":"# Test Infrastructure: run-all-tests.sh Runner Script

## Purpose
Create a unified test runner that executes all tests with detailed logging, summary reporting, and CI-friendly output.

## Features

### 1. Test Discovery
- Auto-discover all test_*.sh files in scripts/tests/
- Support for --filter to run specific tests
- Support for --exclude to skip tests
- List mode: show discovered tests without running

### 2. Execution
- Run each test in isolated subshell
- Capture stdout and stderr separately
- Capture exit codes
- Enforce timeout per test (configurable, default 60s)
- Support for parallel execution (--parallel N)

### 3. Logging
- Per-test log files in logs/tests/YYYY-MM-DD/
- Structured JSON summary at end
- Human-readable progress output to stderr
- Color-coded results (pass/fail/skip)
- Verbose mode shows full test output

### 4. Reporting
- Total tests: run, passed, failed, skipped
- Duration per test and total
- List of failed tests with error snippets
- Exit code: 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail

### 5. CI Integration
- `--ci` mode for GitHub Actions
- JUnit XML output (--junit=path)
- TAP output format (--tap)
- Non-zero exit on any failure

## Sample Output
```
=== dsr Test Suite ===
Running 12 test files...

  test_logging.sh          8 tests  PASS  (0.4s)
  test_config.sh          12 tests  PASS  (1.2s)
  test_build_state.sh     15 tests  PASS  (0.8s)
  test_github.sh           6 tests  SKIP  (network)
  test_signing.sh          4 tests  FAIL  (0.3s)
    - test_sign_missing_key: Expected exit 1, got 0

=== Summary ===
Tests:   45 run, 39 passed, 1 failed, 5 skipped
Time:    3.2s

Failed tests:
  scripts/tests/test_signing.sh::test_sign_missing_key
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Discovers and runs all test_*.sh files
- [ ] Produces per-test log files
- [ ] Outputs JSON summary
- [ ] Supports --ci mode
- [ ] Returns non-zero on any failure
- [ ] Supports timeout enforcement
- [ ] Documents all options in --help","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:23.062043997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:36:42.178380240Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lm","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:23.062043997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-36r","title":"Unit tests for quality_gates.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for quality_gates.sh Module

## Module Overview
quality_gates.sh provides pre-release quality checks:
- `quality_run_checks` - Execute all configured checks
- `quality_parse_config` - Read checks from repos.d/*.yaml
- `quality_format_results` - Output results in JSON/human formats
- `quality_should_skip` - Handle --skip-checks flag

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Check Execution Tests
- Run single check command (e.g., `cargo test`)
- Run multiple checks in sequence
- Capture stdout/stderr per check
- Measure duration per check
- Handle check timeout

### 2. Config Parsing Tests
- Parse checks array from YAML
- Handle missing checks section (no-op)
- Validate check command format
- Support check-specific options

### 3. Result Formatting Tests
- JSON output matches schema
- Human output includes pass/fail indicators
- Duration shown per check
- Exit code summary at end

### 4. Skip Logic Tests
- --skip-checks skips all checks
- --skip-checks=lint skips specific check
- --dry-run shows planned checks
- Non-interactive mode continues on failure

### 5. Error Handling Tests
- Check command not found → clear error
- Check returns non-zero → record failure
- Timeout reached → record timeout
- Continue after failure (report at end)

## Test Approach
- Create temp repos.d with test configs
- Use simple check commands (true, false, sleep)
- Verify JSON schema compliance
- Test all exit code scenarios","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:56.002943319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:21:59.397263745Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:21:59.396976894Z","close_reason":"Implemented 39 tests (15 pass, 24 skip when yq unavailable). Tests cover qg_get_checks, _qg_run_single_check, qg_run_checks with options, exit codes, and edge cases.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-36r","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:56.002943319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3cn","title":"Unit tests for github.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for github.sh Module

## Module Overview
github.sh provides GitHub API access with caching and rate limiting:
- `gh_check` - Verify gh CLI + authentication
- `gh_check_token` - Validate GITHUB_TOKEN
- `gh_api` - Make API requests with caching
- `gh_workflow_runs` - List workflow runs
- `gh_releases` - List releases
- `gh_create_release` - Create a release
- `gh_upload_asset` - Upload release asset

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Authentication Tests
- `gh_check` succeeds when gh is authenticated
- `gh_check` fails gracefully when gh not installed
- `gh_check` fails gracefully when not authenticated
- `gh_check_token` validates GITHUB_TOKEN format
- `gh_check_token` detects expired/invalid tokens (via API call)

### 2. Caching Tests
- Cache directory created under XDG_CACHE_HOME
- First request creates cache entry
- Second request within TTL uses cache
- Request after TTL expiry fetches fresh
- ETag-based validation (304 Not Modified)
- Cache key includes endpoint parameters

### 3. Rate Limiting Tests
- Rate limit detection via response headers
- Retry with backoff on 429 response
- Maximum retry count enforced (3)
- Rate limit warning logged

### 4. Error Handling Tests
- Network errors produce clear messages
- 404 responses handled correctly
- 401 (auth) errors trigger re-auth hint
- 500 errors trigger retry logic

### 5. JSON Output Tests
- `--json` mode returns valid JSON
- Error responses follow envelope schema
- Success responses include metadata

## Test Approach
- Test validation/formatting logic WITHOUT live API calls
- Use skip markers for tests requiring network
- Cache tests use temp directory
- Rate limit tests use mock time if needed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:45.881678501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:14:18.130245753Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:14:18.129628029Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cn","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:45.881678501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
